items.title: Israel attacks Iran's South Pars gas field again
items.description: Israeli airstrikes have hit two petrochemical plants in Iran's South Pars gas field complex. It is the second time Israel has targeted the key energy complex since the war started. The previous attack, on March 18, triggered retaliatory strikes by Iran on energy infrastructure across the region. We look at why the gas field is so important for Iran and the world. Plus, Pakistan has offered public transport to people to cushion the impact of the Middle Eastern energy crisis.

items.title: 'Really feeling the love' - Savannah Guthrie returns to NBC as search for mother goes on
items.description: Nancy Guthrie disappeared from her home in Tucson, Arizona, in what authorities believe was an abduction.

items.title: Cairo goes dark as Egypt struggles to cope with fall out from Iran war
items.description: Normally bustling with activity until the early hours of the morning, the streets of Cairo have gone dark. Soaring fuel costs have pushed the Egyptian government to issue a month-long early-closing order. Now shops are shuttered at 9pm during the week and 10pm on weekends.

items.title: As Vance heads to Hungary, what are the ties between MAGA and Orbán?
items.description: US Vice President JD Vance arrived in Hungary's capital Tuesday in a bid to turn the tide of an election campaign where long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, a close ally of President Donald Trump, is trailing in the polls. France 24's Oliver Farry takes a look at the ties between the US MAGA movement and Hungary's far-right leader.

items.title: JD Vance visits Viktor Orban ahead of Hungary's high-stakes parliamentary elections
items.description: US Vice President JD Vance flew to Hungary on Monday to back nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban ahead of a closely contested parliamentary election. Vance said talks would cover bilateral ties, Europe and Ukraine. He is due to meet Orban and deliver a speech highlighting US-Hungary relations, his office said.

items.title: APC South-East Zonal leadership replies Deputy National Chair Nwoye over Arise TV remarks

items.title: Greek ministers resign over EU farming subsidy scandal
items.description: A scandal over the misuse of EU subsidies has triggered ministerial resignations in Greece. Beneficiaries allegedly made claims for land and livestock they did not own, waved through by lawmakers seeking votes.

items.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
items.description: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

items.title: Family angered by sentence after teenager left to die 'like he was nothing'
items.description: The father of a teenager who was fatally struck while riding an e-scooter near Newcastle says the consequences of driving while distracted are catastrophic.

items.title: 'I'm not worried about it': Donald Trump's shifting rhetoric on the Iran war
items.description: Donald Trump's increasingly erratic language means that a US president's word — which would have once been regarded with gravitas — holds perhaps only the same weight as the word of the "evil" regime he says he has obliterated several times.

items.title: Old students demand clarification as Nigerian govt initiates 18 PPP projects at FGC Kano
items.description: The alumni body is seeking clarification on the terms of the PPP, citing the need to protect the institution’s landed assets and ensure that any development serves the long-term academic interests of the college. items.title: Nigerian worshippers celebrate Easter under tight security after deadly attack

items.title: Russians living in exile cope with grief far from home

items.title: 'Vladimir Putin absolutely nervous' about Hungary's election, expert says
items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Dr Marc Roscoe Loustau, Affiliated Fellow at the Central European University, says that "next to Viktor Orban, the person who stands to lose the most in case of a Fidesz party loss is Vladimir Putin". According to press reports quoting intelligence officials, the powerful explosives found near a Serbian gas pipeline to Hungary may be part of a "false flag" operation to influence the upcoming election.
